Calculate the 'Cross-Correlation' (The Similarity Matrix) # # This mimics exactly what the network plot draws # sim_matrix <- cor(diablo_res$X$microbiome, diablo_res$X$metabolites) #  # # Convert to a list and find the links for S. aureus specifically # sa_name <- grep("aureus", colnames(diablo_res$X$microbiome), value = TRUE) # if(length(sa_name) > 0) { #   sa_correlations <- sort(sim_matrix[sa_name, ], decreasing = TRUE) #   cat("\n--- Top Metabolites correlated with S. aureus ---\n") #   print(head(sa_correlations, 10)) # } #  # png("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/DIABLO/Metab_Loadings_Comp1.png", width = 1200, height = 1200, res = 150) # # Plot the top 10 metabolites and how they contribute to separating LES vs NON_LES # plotLoadings(diablo_res, block = "metabolites", comp = 1, ndisplay = 10, method = 'median') # dev.off() #  # png("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/DIABLO/Microb_Loadings_Comp1.png", width = 1200, height = 1200, res = 150) # # Plot the top 10 metabolites and how they contribute to separating LES vs NON_LES # plotLoadings(diablo_res,  #              block = "microbiome",  #              comp = 1,  #              ndisplay = 10,  #              method = 'median',  #              title = "Microbiome Loadings on Comp 1") # dev.off() #  # # 1. Prepare the data # # We use sa_name which we found in the previous step # plot_data <- data.frame( #   SA_Abundance = species_sub[, sa_name], #   Glutamic_Acid = nmr_sub[, "Glutamic acid"], #   Status = metadata_for_mae[rownames(species_sub), "lesional"] # ) #  # # 2. Run the Linear Model to get stats for the label # fit <- lm(Glutamic_Acid ~ SA_Abundance, data = plot_data) # summary_fit <- summary(fit) # p_val <- summary_fit$coefficients[2,4] # r_sq  <- summary_fit$r.squared #  # # 3. Create the ggplot object # reg_plot <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = SA_Abundance, y = Glutamic_Acid)) + #   geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "gray", linetype = "dashed", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.1) + #   geom_point(aes(color = Status), size = 4, alpha = 0.8) + #   scale_color_manual(values = c("LES" = "firebrick", "NON_LES" = "dodgerblue")) + #   labs(title = "Statistical Validation: S. aureus vs Glutamic Acid", #        subtitle = paste0("Linear Regression: R² = ", round(r_sq, 3),  #                         " | p-value = ", format.pval(p_val, digits = 3)), #        x = "S. aureus Abundance (Metagenomics)", #        y = "Glutamic acid (NMR Intensity)", #        color = "Skin Status") + #   theme_bw() + #   theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")) #  # # 4. Show the plot in RStudio # print(reg_plot) #  # # 5. SAVE the plot using ggsave (This works for ggplot objects!) # ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/DIABLO/Regression_SA_vs_GlutamicAcid.png",  #        plot = reg_plot, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300) #  # print(summary_fit$coefficients) #  # # Extract all loadings for the microbiome # all_microbe_loadings <- diablo_res$loadings$microbiome[,1] #  # # Find S. aureus specifically # sa_val <- all_microbe_loadings[grep("aureus", names(all_microbe_loadings))] #  # # Print the value # cat("The loading for S. aureus is:", sa_val, "\n") #  # # Look at the bottom of the list (the negative loadings) # cat("\nBottom 10 species (Negative Loadings):\n") # print(sort(all_microbe_loadings)[1:10]) #  # # Define the candidates based on your high-loading bars # candidates <- c("Alanine", "Leucine", "Glutamic acid") #  # # Run the validation loop # for (metab in candidates) { #   # Sync data #   df_temp <- data.frame( #     SA = species_sub[, sa_name], #     Metabolite = nmr_sub[, metab] #   ) #    #   # Run regression #   fit_temp <- lm(Metabolite ~ SA, data = df_temp) #   stats <- summary(fit_temp)$coefficients #    #   cat("\n--- Testing S. aureus vs", metab, "---\n") #   cat("P-value:", stats[2,4], "\n") #   cat("R-squared:", summary(fit_temp)$r.squared, "\n") #    #   # If it's a winner, save the plot #   if(stats[2,4] < 0.10) { # Saving anything even 'trending' #     p <- ggplot(df_temp, aes(x = SA, y = Metabolite)) + #       geom_point(color = "darkred", size = 3) + #       geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black") + #       labs(title = paste("S. aureus vs", metab), #            subtitle = paste("p =", round(stats[2,4], 4))) + #       theme_minimal() #      #     ggsave(paste0("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/DIABLO/Regression_SA_vs_", metab, ".png"), plot = p) #   } # } #  # # 1. Prepare the Data # plot_data_ala <- data.frame( #   SA_Abundance = species_sub[, sa_name], #   Alanine = nmr_sub[, "Alanine"], #   Status = metadata_for_mae[rownames(species_sub), "lesional"] # ) #  # # 2. Run the Model (for the subtitle stats) # fit_ala <- lm(Alanine ~ SA_Abundance, data = plot_data_ala) # sum_ala <- summary(fit_ala) #  # # 3. Create the Plot # ala_plot <- ggplot(plot_data_ala, aes(x = SA_Abundance, y = Alanine)) + #   # Add the regression line #   geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", linetype = "dashed", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.1) + #   # Color points by Status (LES vs NON_LES) #   geom_point(aes(color = Status), size = 4, alpha = 0.8) + #   # Professional colors #   scale_color_manual(values = c("LES" = "firebrick", "NON_LES" = "dodgerblue")) + #   labs(title = "Validation: S. aureus vs Alanine", #        subtitle = paste0("R² = ", round(sum_ala$r.squared, 3),  #                         " | p-value = ", round(sum_ala$coefficients[2,4], 4)), #        x = "S. aureus Abundance", #        y = "Alanine Concentration (NMR)", #        color = "Skin Status") + #   theme_bw() #  # # 4. Save it # ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/DIABLO/Regression_SA_vs_Alanine_Colored.png",  #        plot = ala_plot, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300) #  # # Display in RStudio # print(ala_plot) #  #  # # 1. Force the EASI scores to be numeric # easi_scores <- as.numeric(as.character(metadata_for_mae[rownames(nmr_sub), "local_EASI_intensity"])) #  # # 2. Force Alanine to be numeric # alanine_vals <- as.numeric(nmr_sub[, "Alanine"]) #  # # 3. Run the correlation # cor_result <- cor.test(alanine_vals, easi_scores, method = "spearman") # Spearman is safer for clinical scores #  # # 4. Print the result # print(cor_result)`

### Sensitivity & Robustness Checks

`{# {r} # # ============================================================================== # # SENSITIVITY ANALYSIS — MOFA+ WITHOUT OUTLIERS (N=15) # # =========== # # 1. PREPARE THE "CLEAN" DATA (N=14) # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # patients_to_drop <- c("GYECZ1187", "GYECZ1197") # keep_logic <- !(mae_data$eg_id %in% patients_to_drop) # mae_clean <- mae_data[, keep_logic, ] #  # # --- CRITICAL: CLEAN DATA BEFORE INITIALISING MOFA --- # # Identify and remove pathways that have ZERO variance in these 14 samples # pw_data <- assay(mae_clean[["pathways"]]) # bad_features <- which(apply(pw_data, 1, var) == 0) #  # if(length(bad_features) > 0) { #   cat("Warning: Found", length(bad_features), "pathways with zero variance. Removing...\n") #   mae_clean <- mae_clean[-bad_features, , "pathways"] # } #  # # 2. INITIALISE BASE OBJECT (Using the now-clean mae_clean) # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # mofa_clean_base <- create_mofa(mae_clean) #  # data_opts_clean  <- get_default_data_options(mofa_clean_base) # model_opts_clean <- get_default_model_options(mofa_clean_base) # train_opts_clean <- get_default_training_options(mofa_clean_base) #  # data_opts_clean$scale_views         <- TRUE # model_opts_clean$num_factors        <- 3  # The absolute minimum for stability # train_opts_clean$convergence_mode   <- "fast" # train_opts_clean$maxiter            <- 500 #  # mofa_clean_base <- prepare_mofa( #   object           = mofa_clean_base, #   data_options     = data_opts_clean, #   model_options    = model_opts_clean, #   training_options = train_opts_clean # ) #  # # 3. STABILITY LOOP  # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # n_inits          <- 3 # best_elbo_clean  <- -Inf # best_model_clean <- NULL #  # for (i in 1:n_inits) { #   cat("\n--- Running Sensitivity Init", i, "of", n_inits, "---\n") #    #   # Ensure Python options are fresh for each run #   tmp_obj <- mofa_clean_base #   tmp_obj@training_options$seed <- 123 + i #    #   try({ #     trained <- run_mofa(tmp_obj, use_basilisk = TRUE) #     current_elbo <- as.numeric(tail(trained@training_stats$elbo, 1)) #      #     if (!is.na(current_elbo) && is.finite(current_elbo) && current_elbo > best_elbo_clean) { #       best_elbo_clean  <- current_elbo #       best_model_clean <- trained #       cat(">>> New Best Sensitivity Model! ELBO:", round(best_elbo_clean, 2), "\n") #     } #   }) # } #  # # 4. ROBUSTNESS CHECK — COMPARE WEIGHTS ACROSS ALL VIEWS & FACTORS 1-2 # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # model_full <- readRDS("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/mofa_model_10_factors_FINAL.rds") #  # cat("ROBUSTNESS SCORES (Full vs Sensitivity)\n") #  # for (f in 1:2) { #   for (v in c("species", "metabolites", "pathways")) { #     w_full  <- get_weights(model_full,        views = v, factors = f)[[1]] #     w_clean <- get_weights(best_model_clean,  views = v, factors = f)[[1]] #     common  <- intersect(rownames(w_full), rownames(w_clean)) #     r       <- cor(w_full[common, 1], w_clean[common, 1]) #     cat("Factor", f, "-", v, ": r =", round(r, 3), "\n") #   } # } #  # # 5. OUTLIER REPORT (from Full Model) # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # z_df    <- get_factors(model_full)$group1 # z_scores <- scale(z_df) #  # outlier_indices <- which(abs(z_scores) > 3, arr.ind = TRUE) # outlier_report  <- data.frame( #   Sample  = rownames(z_scores)[outlier_indices[, 1]], #   Factor  = colnames(z_scores)[outlier_indices[, 2]], #   Z_Score = round(z_scores[outlier_indices], 3) # ) #  # write.csv(outlier_report, #           "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/Outlier_Red_Flag_Report.csv", #           row.names = FALSE) #  # cat("\n--- OUTLIER REPORT ---\n") # print(outlier_report) #  # # 6. TOP FEATURE DRIVERS — FACTOR 1 (from Full Model) # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # get_top_features <- function(model, view_name, n = 10) { #   w <- get_weights(model, views = view_name, factors = 1)[[1]] #   top_df <- data.frame( #     Feature = rownames(w), #     Weight  = w[, 1] #   ) #   top_df <- top_df[order(abs(top_df$Weight), decreasing = TRUE), ] #   return(head(top_df, n)) # } #  # top_species     <- get_top_features(model_full, "species",     10) # top_metabolites <- get_top_features(model_full, "metabolites", 10) #  # final_table <- rbind( #   cbind(View = "Microbiome",  top_species), #   cbind(View = "Metabolites", top_metabolites) # ) #  # final_table$Feature <- gsub("s__", "", final_table$Feature) # final_table$Feature <- gsub("\\.",  " ", final_table$Feature) #  # write.csv(final_table, #           "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/Table1_Factor1_Drivers.csv", #           row.names = FALSE) #  # print(final_table) #  # # 7. SAVE BEST SENSITIVITY MODEL # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # saveRDS(best_model_clean, #         "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/mofa_model_SENSITIVITY.rds") #  #  #  # # 1. Manually load the best sensitivity model from your temporary folder # # Use the path shown in your 'Saving model' log line # best_model_clean <- load_model("/var/folders/k2/4rg347055jj2_b20pxbz89100000gn/T//Rtmp6Ann5R/mofa_20260324-162559.hdf5") #  # cat("\n--- SEARCHING FOR ROBUSTNESS MATCH (Full F1 vs Sensitivity) ---\n") #  # # Loop through all 3 factors in the clean model to find where F1 went # for (f_new in 1:3) { #   cat("\nChecking Sensitivity Factor", f_new, ":\n") #   for (v in c("species", "metabolites", "pathways")) { #     w_full  <- get_weights(model_full, views = v, factors = 1)[[1]] #     w_clean <- get_weights(best_model_clean, views = v, factors = f_new)[[1]] #      #     common  <- intersect(rownames(w_full), rownames(w_clean)) #     r <- cor(w_full[common, 1], w_clean[common, 1], method = "spearman") #     cat("  View:", v, "| rho =", round(r, 3), "\n") #   } # } #  # # Check which factor in the sensitivity model is most driven by SA # cat("\n--- S. aureus Loading in Sensitivity Model ---\n") # sa_loadings <- get_weights(best_model_clean, views = "species")[[1]] # sa_row <- sa_loadings["s__Staphylococcus_aureus", ] # print(round(sa_row, 3)) #  # # See the top 5 drivers for the factor where SA is highest # best_sa_factor <- which.max(abs(sa_row)) # cat("\nTop Drivers for Sensitivity Factor", best_sa_factor, ":\n") # plot_top_weights(best_model_clean, view = "species", factor = best_sa_factor, nfeatures = 5) #  #  # # --- heatmap of to SA-associated metabolites across samples-------- #  # # 1. PREPARE THE DATA FRAME MANUALLY # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # # Extract colData safely # metadata <- as.data.frame(colData(mae_data)) #  # # Double check if eg_id is actually in the metadata # # If it's missing, we'll use the rownames (Sample IDs) instead # if(!"eg_id" %in% colnames(metadata)){ #     # Fallback: identify outliers by searching for the ID in the Sample Name #     is_outlier <- grepl("GYECZ1187|GYECZ1197", rownames(metadata)) # } else { #     is_outlier <- metadata$eg_id %in% c("GYECZ1187", "GYECZ1197") # } #  # # 2. CREATE THE ANNOTATION DATA FRAME # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # anno_df <- data.frame( #   Status = ifelse(is_outlier, "Outlier", "Main_Cohort"), #   row.names = rownames(metadata) # ) #  # # 3. EXTRACT TOP METABOLITES (FACTOR 1) # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # w_metab <- get_weights(model_full, views = "metabolites", factors = 1)[[1]] # top_15_metabs <- rownames(w_metab)[order(abs(w_metab[, 1]), decreasing = TRUE)][1:15] #  # metab_data <- assay(mae_data[["metabolites"]])[top_15_metabs, ] # metab_scaled <- t(scale(t(metab_data))) # Z-score #  # # 4. PLOT # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # library(pheatmap) # pheatmap(metab_scaled, #          main = "Factor 1 Metabolite Drivers (N=17)", #          annotation_col = anno_df, #          color = colorRampPalette(c("navy", "white", "firebrick3"))(50), #          cluster_cols = TRUE, # This will show if outliers group together #          show_colnames = TRUE, #          border_color = NA)`

`{# {r} # # 1. LOAD MODELS # model_full  <- readRDS("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/mofa_model_10_factors_FINAL.rds") # model_clean <- readRDS("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/mofa_model_SENSITIVITY.rds") #  #  # # 2. CALCULATE ROBUSTNESS (Species View) # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # w_full  <- get_weights(model_full, views="species", factors=1)[[1]] # w_clean <- get_weights(model_clean, views="species", factors=1)[[1]] # common  <- intersect(rownames(w_full), rownames(w_clean)) #  # robustness_species <- cor(w_full[common, 1], w_clean[common, 1]) #  # # 3. CALCULATE ROBUSTNESS (Metabolites View) # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # w_full_met  <- get_weights(model_full, views="metabolites", factors=1)[[1]] # w_clean_met <- get_weights(model_clean, views="metabolites", factors=1)[[1]] # common_met  <- intersect(rownames(w_full_met), rownames(w_clean_met)) #  # robustness_metab <- cor(w_full_met[common_met, 1], w_clean_met[common_met, 1]) #  # # 4. PRINT SUMMARY REPORT # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # cat("\n--- PIPELINE ROBUSTNESS REPORT ---\n") # cat("Species Factor 1 Correlation:     ", round(robustness_species, 3), "\n") # cat("Metabolites Factor 1 Correlation: ", round(robustness_metab, 3), "\n") # cat("----------------------------------\n") #  # if(robustness_species > 0.7) { #   cat("RESULT: HIGH ROBUSTNESS. Factor 1 is a valid Cohort Trend.\n") # } else { #   cat("RESULT: LOW ROBUSTNESS. Factor 1 is driven by individual extremes.\n") # }`

`{# {r} # # 1. Select Top Features for each view based on Factor 1 weights # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # get_top_feats <- function(model, view, factor, n=10) { #   w <- get_weights(model, views=view, factors=factor)[[1]] #   abs_w <- sort(abs(w[,1]), decreasing = TRUE) #   return(names(abs_w)[1:n]) # } #  # top_sp  <- get_top_feats(model_full, "species", 1, 10) # top_met <- get_top_feats(model_full, "metabolites", 1, 10) # top_path <- get_top_feats(model_full, "pathways", 1, 10) #  # # 2. Extract and Combine Data # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # # We'll use the scale=TRUE data from the model # all_data <- list() # for(v in c("species", "metabolites", "pathways")) { #   feats <- if(v=="species") top_sp else if(v=="metabolites") top_met else top_path #   all_data[[v]] <- t(model_full@data[[v]][[1]][feats, ]) # } # combined_mat <- do.call(cbind, all_data) #  # # 3. Final Publication Plot # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # # Sort samples by Factor 1 score to show the 'gradient' # f1_scores <- get_factors(model_full)$group1[,1] # combined_mat <- combined_mat[order(f1_scores), ] #  # png("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/FINAL_Integration_Heatmap.png",  #     width = 1500, height = 900, res = 150) #  # pheatmap(t(combined_mat),  #          cluster_cols = FALSE, # Keep them ordered by Factor 1 #          scale = "row", #          annotation_col = sample_anno,  #          annotation_colors = anno_colours, #          gaps_row = c(10, 20), # Visual gaps between Species/Metab/Path #          main = "Multi-Omic Signature of Factor 1", #          color = colorRampPalette(c("#2166AC", "white", "#D6604D"))(100)) #  # dev.off() #  # # 1. GET ACTIVE FACTORS # # (Remember, MOFA+ may have dropped some inactive factors) # n_factors_full  <- ncol(get_factors(model_full)$group1) # n_factors_clean <- ncol(get_factors(model_clean)$group1) # n_to_check      <- min(n_factors_full, n_factors_clean) #  # robustness_results <- data.frame(Factor = 1:n_to_check, Species_Cor = NA, Metab_Cor = NA) #  # # 2. LOOP THROUGH ALL FACTORS # for (i in 1:n_to_check) { #   # Species Correlation #   w_f_sp <- get_weights(model_full, views="species", factors=i)[[1]] #   w_c_sp <- get_weights(model_clean, views="species", factors=i)[[1]] #   common_sp <- intersect(rownames(w_f_sp), rownames(w_c_sp)) #   robustness_results$Species_Cor[i] <- cor(w_f_sp[common_sp, 1], w_c_sp[common_sp, 1]) #    #   # Metabolites Correlation #   w_f_met <- get_weights(model_full, views="metabolites", factors=i)[[1]] #   w_c_met <- get_weights(model_clean, views="metabolites", factors=i)[[1]] #   common_met <- intersect(rownames(w_f_met), rownames(w_c_met)) #   robustness_results$Metab_Cor[i] <- cor(w_f_met[common_met, 1], w_c_met[common_met, 1]) # } #  # # 3. VIEW THE MAP # print(robustness_results) #  # # 4. SAVE FOR SUPPLEMENTARY DATA # write.csv(robustness_results, "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/Full_Robustness_Report.csv", row.names = FALSE)`

### Visualisations

`{# {r} # # 1. SETUP # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # mofa_to_plot <- readRDS("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/mofa_model_v4_BEST.rds") #  # # Ensure the output directory exists # output_dir <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/" # if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE) #  # # 2. VARIANCE EXPLAINED # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # p1 <- plot_variance_explained(mofa_to_plot, x="view", y="factor") +  #   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) #  # p2 <- plot_variance_explained(mofa_to_plot, plot_total = TRUE) #  # ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "mofa_variance_per_factor_v5.png"),  #        plot = p1, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg="white") # ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "mofa_variance_total_v5.png"),  #        plot = p2, width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg="white") #  # # 3. FACTOR SCORES HEATMAP # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # # Extract factors for our single group # factors <- get_factors(mofa_to_plot)$group1  #  # # Create sample annotation based on row names # # PRO TIP: Check head(rownames(factors)) to ensure the 'NONLES' string is there # sample_anno <- data.frame( #   Status = ifelse(grepl("NONLES", rownames(factors)), "NONLES", "LES"), #   row.names = rownames(factors) # ) #  # anno_colours <- list(Status = c(LES = "#E41A1C", NONLES = "#377EB8")) #  # png(paste0(output_dir, "mofa_heatmap_v5.png"),  #     width = 1300, height = 800, res = 120, bg = "white") #  # pheatmap(factors, #          annotation_row = sample_anno, #          annotation_colors = anno_colours, #          cluster_rows = TRUE, #          cluster_cols = FALSE, #          scale = "column",  #          color = colorRampPalette(c("#2166AC", "white", "#D6604D"))(100), #          main = "MOFA+ Factor Scores per Sample", #          fontsize = 12, #          cellwidth = 50) # dev.off() #  # # Plot the top 15 features (microbes or pathways) for Factor 1 # p_weights <- plot_top_weights(mofa_to_plot,  #                               view = "pathways",  #                               factor = 1,  #                               nfeatures = 15) # ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "mofa_top_pathways_weights_F1.png"), p_weights) #  # # 1. DEFINE THE FACTORS YOU WANT TO PLOT # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # # We check how many factors are actually in your model (likely 7 based on your output) # active_factors <- 1:ncol(get_factors(model_full)$group1) #  # # 2. THE MULTI-FACTOR LOOP # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # for (f in active_factors) { #    #   cat("\n--- Generating Heatmap for Factor", f, "---") #    #   # A. Select top 10 features for THIS factor (f) #   top_sp   <- names(sort(abs(get_weights(model_full, views="species", factors=f)[[1]][,1]), decreasing=T))[1:10] #   top_met  <- names(sort(abs(get_weights(model_full, views="metabolites", factors=f)[[1]][,1]), decreasing=T))[1:10] #   top_path <- names(sort(abs(get_weights(model_full, views="pathways", factors=f)[[1]][,1]), decreasing=T))[1:10] #    #   # B. Extract Data #   list_data <- list( #     Microbiome  = model_full@data$species[[1]][top_sp, ], #     Metabolites = model_full@data$metabolites[[1]][top_met, ], #     Pathways    = model_full@data$pathways[[1]][top_path, ] #   ) #   combined_mat <- do.call(rbind, list_data) #    #   # C. Re-order samples by the current factor (f) scores #   # This ensures each heatmap shows a smooth transition for THAT factor #   f_scores <- get_factors(model_full)$group1[, f] #   combined_mat <- combined_mat[, order(f_scores)] #    #   # D. Sync Annotation #   sample_anno_ordered <- sample_anno[colnames(combined_mat), , drop=FALSE] #   rownames(combined_mat) <- clean_label(rownames(combined_mat)) #    #   # E. Save File #   file_name <- paste0("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/Heatmap_Factor_", f, ".png") #    #   png(file_name, width = 2000, height = 1200, res = 150) #    #   pheatmap(combined_mat,  #            cluster_cols = FALSE,  #            cluster_rows = TRUE,  #            scale = "row",  #            annotation_col = sample_anno_ordered,  #            annotation_colors = anno_colours, #            gaps_row = c(10, 20), #            main = paste("Integrated Multi-Omic Signature: Factor", f), #            color = colorRampPalette(c("#2166AC", "white", "#B2182B"))(100), #            fontsize_row = 10, #            cellwidth = 30) #    #   dev.off() # } #  # cat("\n--- ALL HEATMAPS SAVED TO DESKTOP ---") #  # # Simple T-test for each factor against Status # # 1. Create a clean, dedicated Data Frame from your master_table # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # # We pull from master_table and ensure it's a data.frame, not a character vector # meta_to_add <- master_table %>% #   select(sample = NMR_sample_id, Status = lesional) %>% #   distinct() %>% #   as.data.frame() # Force the 'data.frame' class to avoid signature errors #  # # 2. Filter to match the samples actually in your model # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # # This handles the sample difference (N=17 or N=15) automatically # current_samples <- samples_names(model_full)[[1]] # meta_to_add <- meta_to_add[meta_to_add$sample %in% current_samples, ] #  # # 3. Assign using the correct MOFA2 function # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # # Wrapping in as.data.frame resolves the 'signature = character' error # samples_metadata(model_full) <- as.data.frame(meta_to_add) #  # # 4. Final Verification # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # cat("Success! Group counts in model:\n") # print(table(model_full@samples_metadata$Status)) #  # # 5. Re-run Significance Test for all 10 Factors # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # f_mat <- get_factors(model_full)$group1 # status_vec <- as.factor(model_full@samples_metadata$Status) #  # sig_results <- data.frame( #   Factor = paste0("Factor ", 1:ncol(f_mat)), #   P_Value = sapply(1:ncol(f_mat), function(i) { #     # Perform t-test between LES and NONLES for each factor #     t.test(f_mat[,i] ~ status_vec)$p.value #   }) # ) #  # sig_results$Significant <- ifelse(sig_results$P_Value < 0.05, "YES", "no") # print(sig_results) #  #  # # 1. Align SA abundance from master_table to the model factors # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # factors_df <- as.data.frame(get_factors(model_full)$group1) # factors_df$sample <- rownames(factors_df) #  # # Link SA abundance and EASI from your master_table # clinical_data <- master_table %>% #   select(sample = NMR_sample_id, sa_abundance, EASI) %>% #   distinct() #  # factors_df <- factors_df %>% #   left_join(clinical_data, by = "sample") #  # # 2. Correlate all factors with SA abundance # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # sa_correlations <- sapply(1:7, function(i) { #   cor.test(factors_df[[paste0("Factor", i)]], factors_df$sa_abundance, method = "spearman")$p.value # }) #  # # 3. Correlate all factors with EASI # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # easi_correlations <- sapply(1:7, function(i) { #   cor.test(factors_df[[paste0("Factor", i)]], factors_df$EASI, method = "spearman")$p.value # }) #  # # 4. Final Clinical Significance Report # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # clinical_sig <- data.frame( #   Factor = paste0("Factor ", 1:7), #   P_vs_Status = sig_results$P_Value, # From your previous run #   P_vs_SA = round(sa_correlations, 5), #   P_vs_EASI = round(easi_correlations, 5) # ) #  # print(clinical_sig) #  # # 1. EXTRACT FACTOR SCORES # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # f_scores <- as.data.frame(get_factors(model_full)$group1) # f_scores$sample <- rownames(f_scores) #  # # 2. CREATE A CLEAN MAPPING TABLE FROM MASTER_TABLE # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # # We extract only the necessary columns and remove duplicates # clinical_map <- master_table %>% #   select(sample = NMR_sample_id, sa_abundance, Status = lesional) %>% #   distinct() %>% #   filter(!is.na(sample)) #  # # 3. JOIN AND CLEAN # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # # We join the clinical data to the factor scores using the sample IDs # plot_data <- f_scores %>% #   left_join(clinical_map, by = "sample") %>% #   mutate( #     sa_abundance = as.numeric(sa_abundance), #     Factor2 = as.numeric(Factor2), #     Status = as.factor(Status) #   ) #  # # 4. GENERATE THE SCATTER PLOT # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # library(ggplot2) #  # p_f2_sa <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = sa_abundance, y = Factor2, colour = Status)) + #   geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.8) + #   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "black", linetype = "dashed") + #   scale_colour_manual(values = c("LES" = "#E41A1C", "NONLES" = "#377EB8")) + #   labs( #     title = "MOFA+ Factor 2 vs. S. aureus Abundance", #     subtitle = "Significant Multi-Omic Driver (p < 0.00001)", #     x = "S. aureus Abundance (%)", #     y = "Factor 2 Score", #     colour = "Status" #   ) + #   theme_classic() #  # # 5. PRINT AND SAVE # # ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ # print(p_f2_sa) #  # ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/F2_vs_SA_Scatter_MANUAL_FIX.png",  #        plot = p_f2_sa, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white")`

### 4.3. Correlate Factor Scores with S. Aureus Abundance

Correlate MOFA+ factor scores with SA abundance to identify which factor tracks with SA.

**Why do this? -\>** MOFA+ factors are hidden variables capturing a pattern of co-variation across data. When factors is correlated with SA abundance and score consistently high it means samples from SA high patients tend to score high on factor X (vice versa for low SA). Which would mean the biological variation captured by factor X tracks with SA abundance. Finding out this would help you answer the biological story of what changes in the microbiome and metabolome when SA is high.

So the Spearman correlation are between each factor's score with SA and EASI (lesionality). For each factor (1-4), across all 18 samples, asking if that samples score on that factor track with how much SA that sample has.

```{r}
# Add SA abundance to factors dataframe # factors_df <- as.data.frame(factors) # factors_df$sa_abundance <- master_table$sa_abundance[ # match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id)
# ]
# factors_df$EASI <- master_table$EASI[
#   match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id) # ] # factors_df$lesional <- master_table$lesional[ # match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id)
# ]
#
# # -------- F1&2 Correlations with SA & EASI -------------
# # Spearman correlations with SA - Factors 1 and 2 only
# sa_cors <- cor(factors_df[, 1:2],
#                factors_df$sa_abundance, # method = "spearman", # use = "complete.obs") # # print(sa_cors) # # Spearman correlations with EASI - Factors 1 and 2 only # easi_cors <- cor(factors_df[, 1:2], # factors_df$EASI,
#                  method = "spearman",
#                  use = "complete.obs")
#
# print(easi_cors)
# # Run cor.test for each factor against SA and EASI
# # p values for Factors 1 and 2 only
# for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:2)) {
#   cat("\n---", f, "---\n")
#
#   sa_test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]],
#                       factors_df$sa_abundance, # method = "spearman", exact=FALSE) # cat("SA: rho =", round(sa_test$estimate, 3),
#       "  p =", round(sa_test$p.value, 3), "\n") ## easi_test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]], # factors_df$EASI,
#                         method = "spearman", exact=FALSE)
#   cat("EASI: rho =", round(easi_test$estimate, 3), # " p =", round(easi_test$p.value, 3), "\n")
# }
#
# #------- F1&2 Correlations with all Clincal Variables-------
# # Select numeric clinical variables
# clinical_vars <- master_table %>%
#   select(sa_abundance, EASI, age, TEWL_mean, pH_mean,
#          weeks_since_last_TCS_or_TCI) %>%
#   mutate(
#     TEWL_mean = as.numeric(gsub("none|not_done", NA, TEWL_mean)),
#     pH_mean = as.numeric(gsub("none|not_done", NA, pH_mean)),
#     EASI = as.numeric(EASI),
#     age = as.numeric(age)
#   )
# # Match to factor scores by NMR sample ID
# clinical_vars <- clinical_vars[
#   match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id), # ] # # # Correlate all clinical variables against Factors 1 and 2 # for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:2)) { # cat("\n======", f, "======\n") # for(v in colnames(clinical_vars)) { # test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]], clinical_vars[[v]], # method = "spearman", exact = FALSE) # cat(v, ": rho =", round(test$estimate, 3),
#         "  p =", round(test$p.value, 3), "\n") # } # } # # # Add lesional status to factors_df and plot Factor 1 by lesional status # F1_lesional <- ggplot(factors_df, aes(x = lesional, y = Factor1, fill = lesional)) + # geom_boxplot() + # geom_jitter(width = 0.1) + # labs(title = "Factor 1 scores by lesional status") + # theme_classic() # # # Test it statistically # wilcox.test(Factor1 ~ lesional, data = factors_df) # # ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/F1_by_Lesionality3.png", # plot = F1_lesional, # width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300) # # # test all factors correlation with lesionality # for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:4)) { # test <- wilcox.test(factors_df[[f]] ~ factors_df$lesional)
#   cat(f, ": p =", round(test$p.value, 3), "\n") # } #
```

```         

Exploring the factors

# {r}
# plot_variance_explained(mofa_obj, max_r2 =10)
# 
# 
# # --- Top Weights ----------
# # Plots top n features with the highest absolute loading value
# # for a specific factor
# # Good for identifying the main drivers of a factor
# # high absolute loading value >0.8
# 
# target_factors <- 1:4
# target_views <- c("species", "metabolites")
# 
# for (f in target_factors) {
#   for (v in target_views) {
#     
#     # 1. Create the Top Weights plot
#     p <- plot_top_weights(mofa_obj, 
#                           view = v, 
#                           factor = f, 
#                           nfeatures = 10, 
#                           scale = TRUE) + 
#       ggtitle(paste("Top 10", v, "Weights for Factor", f)) +
#       theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10)) # Adjust font here
#     
#     # 2. Create a dynamic filename
#     file_name <- paste0("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/top_", v, "_weights_F", f, ".png")
#     
#     # 3. Save
#     ggsave(file_name, plot = p, width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 300)
#     
#     message(paste("Saved plot for Factor", f, "View", v))
#   }
# }
```

Three-view model with pathways

```{r}
# {r} # # ------- Post-hoc Analysis - 3 View Model ------- #  # # Add clinical variables to factors dataframe # factors_df <- as.data.frame(factors) # factors_df$sa_abundance <- master_table$sa_abundance[ #   match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id)] # factors_df$EASI <- master_table$EASI[ #   match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id)] # factors_df$lesional <- master_table$lesional[ #   match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id)] #  # # -------- Correlations with all factors (not just 1&2) ------------- # # Now we know Factors 1 and 2 are SA-associated, test all 4 # for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:4)) { #   cat("\n---", f, "---\n") #    #   sa_test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]], #                       factors_df$sa_abundance, #                       method = "spearman", exact = FALSE) #   cat("SA:   rho =", round(sa_test$estimate, 3), #       "  p =", round(sa_test$p.value, 3), "\n") #    #   easi_test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]], #                         factors_df$EASI, #                         method = "spearman", exact = FALSE) #   cat("EASI: rho =", round(easi_test$estimate, 3), #       "  p =", round(easi_test$p.value, 3), "\n") # } #  # # -------- Correlations with all clinical variables ------- # clinical_vars <- master_table %>% #   select(sa_abundance, EASI, age, TEWL_mean, pH_mean, #          weeks_since_last_TCS_or_TCI) %>% #   mutate( #     TEWL_mean = as.numeric(gsub("none|not_done", NA, TEWL_mean)), #     pH_mean = as.numeric(gsub("none|not_done", NA, pH_mean)), #     EASI = as.numeric(EASI), #     age = as.numeric(age) #   ) # clinical_vars <- clinical_vars[ #   match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id), ] #  # # Test all 4 factors against all clinical variables # for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:4)) { #   cat("\n======", f, "======\n") #   for(v in colnames(clinical_vars)) { #     test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]], clinical_vars[[v]], #                      method = "spearman", exact = FALSE) #     cat(v, ": rho =", round(test$estimate, 3), #         "  p =", round(test$p.value, 3), "\n") #   } # } #  # # -------- Lesionality ------- # F1_lesional <- ggplot(factors_df, aes(x = lesional, y = Factor1,  #                                        fill = lesional)) + #   geom_boxplot() + #   geom_jitter(width = 0.1) + #   scale_fill_manual(values = c("LES" = "#F8766D", "NON_LES" = "#00BFC4")) + #   labs(title = "Factor 1 scores by lesional status") + #   theme_classic() #  # wilcox.test(Factor1 ~ lesional, data = factors_df) #  # ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/F1_by_Lesionality_3view.png", #        plot = F1_lesional, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300) #  # # Test all factors against lesionality # for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:4)) { #   test <- wilcox.test(factors_df[[f]] ~ factors_df$lesional) #   cat(f, ": p =", round(test$p.value, 3), "\n") # } #  # # -------- Variance explained ------- # plot_variance_explained(mofa_obj, max_r2 = 10) #  # # -------- Top weights - now includes pathways view ------- # target_factors <- 1:4 # target_views <- c("species", "metabolites", "pathways")  # added pathways #  # for(f in target_factors) { #   for(v in target_views) { #     p <- plot_top_weights(mofa_obj, #                           view = v, #                           factor = f, #                           nfeatures = 10, #                           scale = TRUE) + #       ggtitle(paste("Top 10", v, "Weights for Factor", f)) + #       theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10)) #      #     file_name <- paste0("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/top_", #                         v, "_weights_F", f, "_3view.png") #     ggsave(file_name, plot = p, width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 300) #     message(paste("Saved plot for Factor", f, "View", v)) #   } # } #  # # Factor 1 scores by SA abundance - scatter plot # factors_df$sa_abundance <- as.numeric(factors_df$sa_abundance) #  # p_f1_sa <- ggplot(factors_df, aes(x = sa_abundance, y = Factor1,  #                                    colour = lesional)) + #   geom_point(size = 3) + #   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "black", linetype = "dashed") + #   scale_colour_manual(values = c("LES" = "#E41A1C", "NON_LES" = "#377EB8")) + #   labs(title = "MOFA+ Factor 1 vs SA Abundance", #        x = "SA Abundance (%)", #        y = "Factor 1 Score", #        colour = "Lesional Status") + #   annotate("text", x = max(factors_df$sa_abundance, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.1, #            y = max(factors_df$Factor1, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.9, #            label = "rho = -0.776, p < 0.001", #            hjust = 0, size = 4.5) + #   theme_classic() #  # p_f1_sa #  # ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/F1_by_SA_3view.png", #        plot = p_f1_sa, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
```

## Step 5: Linear **Regressions - Identifying SA High/Low Associated Features**

Identifying molecular signatures driven by SA colonisation, running feature-wise linear regressions across two data modalities: NMR Metabolites (n=38) and microbial pathways (n = 248)

-   **The Model:** treating each metabolite or pathway as a dependent variable to see if their levels differ significantly between the SA high and low groups:

    -   Feature∼SA status+Lesionality+pH

-   **The Predictor:** SA abundance is the variable of interest, so asking... *"*Is there a significant difference in the level of this feature between high SA and low SA samples, independent of pH and skin inflammation?

-   **Covariate Adjustment:**

    -   **pH:** was significant (\<0.05) in upstream confounder checking

    -   **Lesionality:** fixed covariate to make sure associations are driven by SA abundance not just general skin inflammation

-   **Multiple Testing Correction:** to account for high number of simultaneous hypotheses (especially in the 248 microbial pathways), p-values are adjusted using Benjamin-Hochberg (FDR)

-   **Validation:** significant results from the linear regression are cross-validated using a non-parametric test (like **Wilcoxon Rank-Sum**) to ensure the differences between high and low groups are robust and not driven by outliers

```{r}
# Creating the High/Low status based on thresholds
master_table <- master_table %>%
  mutate(SA_status = case_when(
    sa_abundance > 35 ~ "High",
    sa_abundance < 10 ~ "Low",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_  # Samples in the 10-35% range become NA
  ))

# Set 'Low' as the reference for the regression
master_table$SA_status <- factor(master_table$SA_status, levels = c("Low", "High"))

# Count the samples in each SA_status group, including NAs
table(master_table$SA_status, useNA = "always")
```

### 5.1. Linear Regressions

```{r}

# --- 1. Define the Master Regression Function ---
# This function automates: Data matching, Linear Modeling, and FDR Correction
# Mutlivariate linear regression: outcome ~ SA_status + lesional + pH
run_omics_regression <- function(data_matrix, master_df, predictor_col = "SA_status") {
  
  results_list <- list()
  common_ids <- intersect(rownames(data_matrix), master_df$NMR_sample_id)
  
  # Filter master_df to only include matching IDs once to speed up match()
  master_sub <- master_df[master_df$NMR_sample_id %in% common_ids, ]

  for(feature in colnames(data_matrix)) {
    
    # 1. Prepare Data
    # Match the order of master_sub to the order of common_ids (rownames of matrix)
    idx <- match(common_ids, master_sub$NMR_sample_id)
    
    df_temp <- data.frame(
      outcome   = as.numeric(as.character(data_matrix[common_ids, feature])),
      SA_group  = as.factor(master_sub[[predictor_col]][idx]),
      lesional  = as.factor(master_sub$lesional[idx]),
      pH        = as.numeric(as.character(master_sub$pH[idx]))
    )
    
    # 2. Clean Data
    df_temp <- na.omit(df_temp)
    
    # Need at least one sample in each group to run regression!
    if(length(unique(df_temp$SA_group)) < 2 || nrow(df_temp) < 5) next

    # 3. Run Model
    model <- tryCatch({
      lm(outcome ~ SA_group + lesional + pH, data = df_temp)
    }, error = function(e) return(NULL))
    
    # 4. Extract "High" vs "Low"
    if(!is.null(model)) {
      summ <- summary(model)$coefficients
      # Because SA_group is the variable, the row is ALWAYS "SA_groupHigh"
      if("SA_groupHigh" %in% rownames(summ)) {
        res <- as.data.frame(t(summ["SA_groupHigh", ]))
        res$feature <- feature
        results_list[[feature]] <- res
      }
    }
  }
  
  if(length(results_list) == 0) stop("No significant coefficients found. Check group sizes!")
  
  final_df <- do.call(rbind, results_list)
  colnames(final_df) <- c("Estimate", "Std_Error", "t_value", "p_value", "feature")
  final_df$p_adjusted <- p.adjust(final_df$p_value, method = "fdr")
  return(final_df)
}

# 1. Run the SA-Metabolite Regressions
results_metabolites <- run_omics_regression(nmr_matched, master_table, predictor_col="SA_status")

# 2. Run the SA-Pathway Regressions
results_pathways <- run_omics_regression(pathway_matched, master_table, predictor_col="SA_status")

# --- 4. Quick View of Top Hits ---
print("Top SA-Associated Metabolites:")
head(results_metabolites[results_metabolites$p_value < 0.05, ])

print("Top SA-Associated Pathways:")
head(results_pathways[results_pathways$p_value < 0.05, ])

# --- Save Metabolite Results ---
write.csv(results_metabolites, 
          "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/SA_Regressions/SA_Metabolite_Regressions_Full.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Save just significant hits (p < 0.05)
metabolite_hits <- results_metabolites[results_metabolites$p_value < 0.05, ]
write.csv(metabolite_hits, 
          "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/SA_Regressions/SA_Metabolite_Hits_p05.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# --- Save Pathway Results ---
write.csv(results_pathways, 
          "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/SA_Regressions/SA_Pathway_Regressions_Full.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Save significant hits (p < 0.05)
pathway_hits <- results_pathways[results_pathways$p_value < 0.05, ]
write.csv(pathway_hits, 
          "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/SA_Regressions/SA_Pathway_Hits_p05.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r}
# --- 1. Define the Helper Function ---
create_sa_boxplot <- function(df, y_var, title_label) {
  # Remove NAs in the grouping variable so they don't show up in the plot
  plot_data <- df[!is.na(df$SA_status), ]
  
  ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = SA_status, y = .data[[y_var]], fill = SA_status)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 2, alpha = 0.6) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Low" = "#56B4E9", "High" = "#D55E00")) +
    labs(title = title_label, 
         subtitle = "Comparison of SA-High vs SA-Low",
         x = "S. aureus Status", 
         y = "Relative Abundance") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14))
}

# --- 2. Prepare Unique Data Frames for Plotting ---
# Extract metadata columns we need to avoid "duplicate column" errors
meta_cols <- master_table[, c("NMR_sample_id", "SA_status", "lesional", "pH_mean")]

# Prepare Metabolite Data
nmr_df <- as.data.frame(nmr_matched, check.names = FALSE)
nmr_df$NMR_sample_id <- rownames(nmr_df)
plot_master <- merge(nmr_df, meta_cols, by = "NMR_sample_id")

# Prepare Pathway Data
path_df <- as.data.frame(pathway_matched, check.names = FALSE)
path_df$NMR_sample_id <- rownames(path_df)
plot_pathways <- merge(path_df, meta_cols, by = "NMR_sample_id")

# --- 3. Generate and Save Metabolite Plots ---
for(met in metabolite_hits$feature) {
  # Check if the feature actually exists in the plotting data
  if(met %in% colnames(plot_master)) {
    p <- create_sa_boxplot(plot_master, met, paste("Metabolite:", met))
    
    ggsave(filename = paste0("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Regressions/Metabolite_", met, ".png"), 
           plot = p, width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 300)
  }
}

# --- 4. Generate and Save Top 5 Pathway Plots ---
# Sort by p-value to get the most significant ones
top_pathways <- pathway_hits[order(pathway_hits$p_value), ]
top_5_names <- head(top_pathways$feature, 5)

for(path in top_5_names) {
  if(path %in% colnames(plot_pathways)) {
    # Clean the filename for saving (remove slashes/special chars)
    clean_name <- gsub("[^[:alnum:]]", "_", substr(path, 1, 30))
    
    p <- create_sa_boxplot(plot_pathways, path, "Pathway Abundance") +
         labs(subtitle = path)
    
    ggsave(filename = paste0("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Regressions/Pathway_", clean_name, ".png"), 
           plot = p, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  }
}

# 1. Grab Top 5 Metabolites (sorted by p-value)
top_met_df <- metabolite_hits[order(metabolite_hits$p_value), ]
top_met_df <- head(top_met_df, 5)
top_met_df$type <- "Metabolite"

# 2. Grab Top 5 Pathways (sorted by p-value)
top_path_df <- pathway_hits[order(pathway_hits$p_value), ]
top_path_df <- head(top_path_df, 5)
top_path_df$type <- "Pathway"

# 3. Combine them
plot_summary <- rbind(top_met_df, top_path_df)

# Shorten long pathway names so they fit on the graph
plot_summary$feature_short <- substr(plot_summary$feature, 1, 40)



library(stringr) # Needed for wrapping text
# Function to convert p-value to stars
get_stars <- function(p) {
  if (p < 0.001) return("***")
  if (p < 0.01)  return("**")
  if (p < 0.05)  return("*")
  return("ns")
}

# Apply to your summary data
plot_summary$stars <- sapply(plot_summary$p_value, get_stars)

# Create a combined label for the Y-axis (e.g., "Glycerol ***")
plot_summary$labeled_feature <- paste0(plot_summary$feature, " ", plot_summary$stars)

# Filter for top metabolites
plot_met <- plot_summary[plot_summary$type == "Metabolite", ]

meta_top <- ggplot(plot_met, aes(x = Estimate, y = reorder(labeled_feature, Estimate))) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = Estimate - Std_Error, xmax = Estimate + Std_Error), 
                 height = 0.3, linewidth = 1, color = "#D55E00") + 
  geom_point(size = 5, color = "#D55E00") + 
  # Add stars next to the points
  geom_text(aes(label = stars), hjust = -0.5, vjust = -0.5, size = 6, color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Metabolic Shifts: High vs Low SA",
       subtitle = "(* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001)",
       x = "Regression Estimate (NMR Intensity)",
       y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18))

ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Regressions/Metabolites_Significance.png", meta_top, width = 7, height = 4)


# 1. Filter for pathways only
plot_path <- plot_summary[plot_summary$type == "Pathway", ]

# 2. Clean dots to spaces for better wrapping
plot_path$feature_clean <- gsub("\\.", " ", plot_path$feature)

# 3. Create the Plot
ggplot(plot_path, aes(x = Estimate, y = reorder(feature_clean, Estimate))) +
  # Reference line at zero
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  # Error bars (Estimate +/- Std_Error)
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = Estimate - Std_Error, xmax = Estimate + Std_Error), 
                 height = 0.3, linewidth = 1, color = "#009E73") + 
  # Mean points
  geom_point(size = 5, color = "#009E73") + 
  # --- ADD STARS ON ERROR BARS ---
  # hjust = -0.5 pushes it slightly to the right of the error bar end
  geom_text(aes(x = Estimate + Std_Error, label = stars), 
            hjust = -0.3, vjust = 0.4, size = 6, color = "black", fontface = "bold") +
  # -------------------------------
  # Wrap the long pathway names
  scale_y_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 35)) +
  labs(title = "Microbial Pathway Shifts: High vs Low SA",
       subtitle = paste("Significance levels:", "(* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001)"),
       x = "Regression Estimate (Abundance)",
       y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 14, color = "gray30"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold", lineheight = 0.8),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, vjust = -0.5),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    # Increase left margin to accommodate the wrapped names
    plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 50, b = 10, l = 80) 
  )

# 4. Save the high-res image
ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Regressions/Pathways_Significance.png", 
       width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

### 5.2. Spearman's Correlations

Running Spearman's correlations on the whole dataset and then finding overlapping significant features with the linear regressions, to see what is significant in both tests.

This doesn't use the high/low groups like the linear regressions, it works on a continuous scale

```{r}
# --- 1. Define the Spearman Function (Updated) ---
run_spearman_check <- function(data_matrix, master_df, predictor_col = "sa_abundance") {
  results_list <- list()
  
  # Ensure we only use IDs that exist in both datasets
  common_ids <- intersect(rownames(data_matrix), master_df$NMR_sample_id)
  
  for(feature in colnames(data_matrix)) {
    # Match and clean data
    val_outcome <- as.numeric(as.character(data_matrix[common_ids, feature]))
    val_predictor <- as.numeric(as.character(master_df[[predictor_col]][match(common_ids, master_df$NMR_sample_id)]))
    
    # Create a temporary DF to remove NAs safely
    df_temp <- na.omit(data.frame(x = val_predictor, y = val_outcome))
    
    # Only run if we have enough data points (Spearman needs at least 3-5)
    if(nrow(df_temp) > 5) {
      test <- cor.test(df_temp$x, df_temp$y, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
      
      results_list[[feature]] <- data.frame(
        feature = feature,
        rho = as.numeric(test$estimate),
        p_spearman = as.numeric(test$p.value)
      )
    }
  }
  return(do.call(rbind, results_list))
}

# --- 2. Run and Compare Metabolites ---
# Note: Use "sa_abundance" here (the raw % values)
spearman_met <- run_spearman_check(nmr_matched, master_table, predictor_col = "sa_abundance")
overlap_met <- merge(results_metabolites, spearman_met, by = "feature")

# High-Confidence: Significant in Linear Regression AND Spearman
high_conf_met <- overlap_met[overlap_met$p_value < 0.05 & overlap_met$p_spearman < 0.05, ]

# --- 3. Run and Compare Pathways ---
spearman_path <- run_spearman_check(pathway_matched, master_table, predictor_col = "sa_abundance")
overlap_path <- merge(results_pathways, spearman_path, by = "feature")

high_conf_path <- overlap_path[overlap_path$p_value < 0.05 & overlap_path$p_spearman < 0.05, ]

# --- 4. View the High-Confidence List ---
print("--- HIGH-CONFIDENCE METABOLITES ---")
print(high_conf_met[, c("feature", "Estimate", "rho", "p_value", "p_spearman")])

print("--- HIGH-CONFIDENCE PATHWAYS ---")
print(head(high_conf_path[order(high_conf_path$p_value), c("feature", "Estimate", "rho", "p_value", "p_spearman")], 10))

# --- Save High-Confidence Metabolites ---
write.csv(high_conf_met, 
          "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Regressions/High_Confidence_Metabolites_SA.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# --- Save High-Confidence Pathways ---
write.csv(high_conf_path, 
          "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Regressions/High_Confidence_Pathways_SA.csv", row.names = FALSE)


# ------- Visualisations----------
# Reorder features by their Estimate for a cleaner look
high_conf_met$feature <- reorder(high_conf_met$feature, high_conf_met$Estimate)

metab_hits <- ggplot(high_conf_met, aes(x = Estimate, y = reorder(feature, Estimate), fill = Estimate > 0)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.8, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D55E00", "#56B4E9"), 
                    labels = c("Decreased with SA", "Increased with SA"),
                    name = "Direction of Change") +
  labs(title = "Metabolites Associated with S. aureus",
       subtitle = "High-Confidence Hits (Regression & Spearman p < 0.05)",
       x = "Regression Estimate (Effect Size)", 
       y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 20),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.key.size = unit(1, "cm"),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 20, 10, 20)
  )

ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Regressions/SA_Metabolite_Hits_Final.png", 
       width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300)


# 1. Prepare Data: Filter out NAs and clean names
high_conf_path_plot <- high_conf_path[!is.na(high_conf_path$feature), ]
high_conf_path_plot$feature_clean <- gsub("\\.", " ", high_conf_path_plot$feature)

# 2. The Plot
ggplot(high_conf_path_plot, aes(x = Estimate, y = reorder(feature_clean, Estimate), fill = Estimate > 0)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.8, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 40)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D55E00", "#56B4E9"), 
                    labels = c("Decreased with SA", "Increased with SA"),
                    name = "Direction of Change") +
  labs(title = "Microbial Pathway Shifts Associated with S. aureus",
       subtitle = "High-Confidence Pathways (Significant in both Regression & Spearman, p<0.05)",
       x = "Regression Estimate (Effect Size)", 
       y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 20),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 16, color = "gray30"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", lineheight = 0.8),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16),
    
    # --- INCREASE KEY (LEGEND) SIZE HERE ---
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"), # Title of the key
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14),                # Labels in the key
    legend.key.size = unit(1.2, "cm"),                    # Size of the colored boxes
    # ---------------------------------------
    
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 50, b = 10, l = 50) 
  )

# 3. Save with a "landscape" aspect ratio
ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Regressions/SA_Pathway_Hits.png", 
       width = 15, height = 11, dpi = 300)
```

## Step 6: Mediation Analysis

So step 5 shows that certain metabolites/pathways are correlated with S. aureus level. This mediation analysis will see if those metabolites/pathways are actually used by SA to drive skin lesionality.

-   Independent variable = SA level

-   Mediator = metabolites/pathways

-   Outcome = lesional vs non lesional

Will run a loop for each metabolite and pathway to see if any are significant mediators. Using bootstrapped confidence intervals (n=5000 permutations), ending up with an estimate of the indirect effect to build the CI.

Scaled the pathways to handle the tiny abundance numbers. Used logistic regression because lesional is binary.

```{r}
# Load the required library
#if(!require(mediation)) install.packages("mediation")
library(mediation)

# Fix the metadata in plotting objects
plot_master$pH_mean <- as.numeric(as.character(plot_master$pH_mean))
plot_pathways$pH_mean <- as.numeric(as.character(plot_pathways$pH_mean))

# --- 1. Define the Master Mediation Function ---
# This handles both Metabolites (Standard) and Pathways (Scaled)
# 1. Create a list to store results
mediation_summary_list <- list()

# 2. Get the list of significant metabolites (from your earlier results)
# You can use metabolite_hits$feature or high_conf_met$feature
target_metabolites <- metabolite_hits$feature 

print(paste("Starting mediation for", length(target_metabolites), "metabolites..."))

for(met in target_metabolites) {
  tryCatch({
    # Run the stable mediation function we defined earlier
    med_out <- run_sa_mediation_stable(met, plot_master, is_pathway = FALSE)
    
    # Extract the key numbers
    sum_med <- summary(med_out)
    
    mediation_summary_list[[met]] <- data.frame(
      Metabolite = met,
      ACME_Estimate = med_out$d0,
      ACME_P_Value = med_out$d0.p,
      ADE_Estimate = med_out$z0,
      ADE_P_Value = med_out$z0.p,
      Prop_Mediated = med_out$n0,
      Total_Effect = med_out$tau.coef
    )
    
    print(paste("Completed:", met))
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    message(paste("Could not run mediation for", met, ":", e$message))
  })
}

# 3. Combine into one table
mediation_results_table <- do.call(rbind, mediation_summary_list)

# 4. View and Sort by P-value
mediation_results_table <- mediation_results_table[order(mediation_results_table$ACME_P_Value), ]
print(mediation_results_table)

# 5. Save the table
write.csv(mediation_results_table, "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/Mediation/Mediation_Results_Metabolites.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# 1. Create a list to store results
mediation_pathway_list <- list()

# 2. Get the top 5 pathways (already sorted by p-value from your earlier code)
target_pathways <- head(top_pathways$feature, 5)

print(paste("Starting mediation for", length(target_pathways), "pathways..."))

for(path in target_pathways) {
  tryCatch({
    # Run the stable mediation function with is_pathway = TRUE
    med_out <- run_sa_mediation_stable(path, plot_pathways, is_pathway = TRUE)
    
    # Extract the key statistics
    mediation_pathway_list[[path]] <- data.frame(
      Pathway = path,
      ACME_Estimate = med_out$d0,
      ACME_P_Value = med_out$d0.p,
      ADE_Estimate = med_out$z0,
      ADE_P_Value = med_out$z0.p,
      Prop_Mediated = med_out$n0,
      Total_Effect = med_out$tau.coef,
      Samples_Used = med_out$nobs
    )
    
    print(paste("Completed:", path))
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    message(paste("Could not run mediation for", path, ":", e$message))
  })
}

# 3. Combine into one table
mediation_pathway_results <- do.call(rbind, mediation_pathway_list)

# 4. Sort by Mediation P-value (ACME)
mediation_pathway_results <- mediation_pathway_results[order(mediation_pathway_results$ACME_P_Value), ]

# 5. Print to console to inspect
print(mediation_pathway_results)

# 6. Save as CSV to your Project Folder
write.csv(mediation_pathway_results, 
          "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/Mediation/Mediation_Results_Pathways.csv", 
          row.names = FALSE)
```
